verdict.answer_first: A leads with the mechanism in sentence 1: "free memory automatically by tracking reachability"; B starts broader: "automatic memory management".
verdict.decisiveness: A keeps one core idea: "garbage doesn't mean unused — it means unreachable"; B becomes a survey with table plus "Practical takeaways" across JS and Go.
verdict.fabrication: B has more unsupported numeric/specific claims: "default 100", "grown 100%", "sub-millisecond", "twice"; A also says "sub-millisecond" but less often.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save as a prescription: "If you still hold a reference... the collector keeps it alive" gives wall+principle+trap; B lists many tactics.
